paper_title: Microbiome Investigation of Bee Bread and Bee Pollen

methods.description: Process for collecting and preparing bee bread and bee pollen samples from honeybee hives for microbiome analysis.

steps.description: Recruit honeybee Apis mellifera ligustica queen-right colonies from the experimental apiary at Andong National University in spring.
steps.searchable_summary: select and recruit honeybee colonies for study

steps.description: Maintain seven healthy honeybee hives of similar strength and composition for the study.
Provision three hives with oak pollen patties and mark them as A, and three hives with rapeseed pollen patties and mark them as R.
steps.searchable_summary: provision hives with specific pollen patties

steps.description: Install pollen traps in one hive from each group (A and R) and mark them as AT and RT, respectively.
steps.searchable_summary: install pollen traps in selected hives

steps.description: Collect at least 20 g of bee bread from each hive on the 5th day using sterile tweezers and needles.
steps.searchable_summary: collect bee bread samples from hives

steps.description: Store collected bee bread samples at −20°C until further processing.
steps.searchable_summary: store bee bread samples at specified temperature

steps.description: Mix samples collected from each hive well and follow a composite sampling method for analyses.
steps.searchable_summary: mix and prepare composite samples for analysis

steps.description: Collect bee pollen samples from hives AT and RT equipped with pollen traps and store them.
steps.searchable_summary: collect and store bee pollen samples from specific hives
